title: gaultheria procumbens
intro: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as Wintergreen, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to eastern North America. The plant's leaves contain methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it useful for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Wintergreen include relief from headaches, arthritis, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Wintergreen is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and can also be found in some topical creams and ointments.
